summary: I'm Davey Day, the fast-stepping Newboy on his way up in boxing. I've fought many fighters around New York City, including Frankie Segni, Roger Bernard, and Bobby Paoli, and I only lost one pro fight back in 1931. I have a lot of confidence and determination, and I'm aiming to be the champion right here in my hometown. Despite a tough loss to Armstrong, I believe I'll get another chance and come out on top.
cues: fights with Armstrong, Ambers, Segni, Bernard, Paoli

summary: I've beaten Frankie Segni in the ring here in New York City, along with many other fighters. He was one of the many opponents I faced as I climbed the ranks in boxing. Fighting Segni was part of my journey to prove myself and build my reputation locally. I remember those fights well as stepping stones to bigger challenges.

Roger Bernard was another fighter I took on and defeated in New York City. He was among the many boxers I faced as I worked my way up the ranks. Each fight, including the one with Bernard, helped me sharpen my skills and gain confidence. I’ve fought about a hundred others too, but Bernard stands out as one of the notable names.

Bobby Paoli was one of the fighters I beat in my hometown. Taking on Paoli and winning was part of my rise in the boxing world here in New York City. I’ve faced many opponents, but Paoli was definitely among those I conquered on my way up. These victories gave me the confidence to take on bigger names like Armstrong and Ambers.

Armstrong was a tough opponent who I fought and lost to, but only because I couldn’t see properly after my eye swelled shut in the twelfth round. He was a strong fighter with a lot of power and determination, and even though I was handicapped, I gave him a real fight. Armstrong’s a smart guy, reads books, and even apologized to me the day after our fight. I’m confident I’ll get another shot at him soon, hopefully right here in my hometown.

I fought Lou Ambers in New York when he was the champion, but they gave him the decision even though he was badly hurt. Armstrong fought Ambers too and got a bad lip from him, but Ambers still got the win. I respect Ambers as a tough champion, but I’m determined to become champ myself and keep the title right here in my hometown.

New York City is my lucky town where I’ve fought most of my fights and built my career. I dropped only one pro fight here back in 1931 and have beaten many fighters around this city. I want to become champion and keep the title right here in New York, where the fans know me and where I feel at home. It’s the place where my boxing story is unfolding.

Fitzpatrick was the referee who called the fight when my eye swelled shut against Armstrong. I wanted to argue the call and keep fighting, but my manager Pian stopped me. Fitzpatrick’s decision was tough to accept, but I understood it was because I couldn’t see and was at a real disadvantage. I respect the referee’s role even if I felt I could have gone on.

Pian is my co-manager who’s been supporting me through my fights. He’s the one who stopped me from arguing Fitzpatrick’s call when my eye was swollen shut in the Armstrong fight. Pian is also planning to get me another fight with Armstrong at the ballpark here in New York City. He’s a key part of my team and helps me stay focused on my goal to become champion.

Angott is another fighter with that old confidence, just like me. I’m interested in him and think he might get a shot at Armstrong before I do. I respect Angott’s determination and skill, and I’m keeping an eye on how things develop with him. He’s part of the competition I’m watching closely as I plan my next moves in the ring.

I’m scheduled to fight Mantanos soon at the ballpark, and I hope it’ll be a good match. That fight is important because it might lead to another chance at Armstrong. Mantanos is part of the path I’m taking to climb back up and prove I’m ready for the big fights. I’m confident and ready to show what I’ve got in that upcoming bout.

A dame once wrote me a fan letter saying I looked like George Raft, who used to be a pug too. I don’t think I look like him, but it was flattering to be compared to someone like Raft. He’s got a name in the boxing world and beyond, and it’s interesting to be linked to him even if just by a fan’s imagination.
